summary: Feature request to improve SKVertices API by exposing SkVertices::Builder, adding vertex/index count parameters, and adding ReadOnlySpan<T> overloads to CreateCopy for efficient vertex buffer construction without forced array allocations.

repoLinks.description: Closed PR by seclerp adding offset/count overloads — maintainer suggested ReadOnlySpan<T> instead

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SKVertices API is unchanged since the issue was filed. No Builder C API or Span overloads have been added.

analysis.summary: Request to improve SKVertices ergonomics in three ways: (1) expose SkVertices::Builder for efficient mutable construction, (2) add vertex/index count parameters to CreateCopy so shared arrays can be used without slicing, and (3) add ReadOnlySpan<T> overloads. The C API already accepts vertexCount/indexCount but the C# wrapper hides this by deriving counts from array lengths. Builder has no C API at all. A related PR (#2432) was closed with maintainer feedback preferring ReadOnlySpan<T> approach.
analysis.rationale: Classified as enhancement because SKVertices.CreateCopy exists and works, but lacks ergonomic overloads and the Builder pattern. The reporter is a contributor who understands the underlying Skia API gap. Maintainer confirmed Span-based approach is preferred in #2432.

keySignals.text: vertex and index counts are not present in the API
keySignals.interpretation: C# API derives counts from array Length, hiding the C API's existing vertexCount/indexCount parameters.

keySignals.text: This forces user to allocate new arrays frequently since copy start and length can't be controlled
keySignals.interpretation: Performance concern — inability to use array slices causes unnecessary GC pressure in hot paths.

keySignals.text: Binding builder would allow for more efficient vertex buffer construction
keySignals.interpretation: SkVertices::Builder exists in C++ but has no C API shim, so it cannot be exposed to C#.
Maintainer prefers Span-based overloads over offset/count parameters — aligns with modern .NET idioms.

codeInvestigation.finding: CreateCopy takes arrays and derives vertexCount from positions.Length and indexCount from indices.Length. The underlying C API sk_vertices_make_copy already accepts explicit counts, but the C# wrapper hides this.
C API only exposes sk_vertices_make_copy, sk_vertices_ref, sk_vertices_unref. No Builder C API functions exist — would need new sk_vertices_builder_* functions.
Upstream SkVertices::Builder class exists with positions(), texCoords(), colors(), indices() accessors and detach() method. Supports BuilderFlags for optional tex coords and colors.
C implementation simply forwards to SkVertices::MakeCopy. No Builder wrapper exists in the C shim layer.

nextQuestions: Should Builder be exposed as a separate SKVerticesBuilder class or nested inside SKVertices?
What Span overloads should be added — ReadOnlySpan<SKPoint>, ReadOnlySpan<SKColor>, ReadOnlySpan<ushort>?
Should the existing array-based CreateCopy methods be marked [Obsolete] or kept alongside Span versions?

resolution.hypothesis: The SKVertices API needs Span-based CreateCopy overloads (which naturally support slicing) and a new Builder wrapper backed by new C API functions.

proposals.title: Add ReadOnlySpan overloads to CreateCopy
proposals.description: Add CreateCopy overloads accepting ReadOnlySpan<SKPoint>, ReadOnlySpan<SKColor>, ReadOnlySpan<ushort>. This solves the offset/count problem naturally since spans can be sliced. The underlying C API already accepts explicit counts, so no native changes needed.

proposals.title: Expose SkVertices::Builder via C API
proposals.description: Create new C API functions (sk_vertices_builder_new, sk_vertices_builder_positions, sk_vertices_builder_tex_coords, sk_vertices_builder_colors, sk_vertices_builder_indices, sk_vertices_builder_detach, sk_vertices_builder_delete) and wrap them in a C# SKVerticesBuilder class. Larger effort but enables mutable construction pattern.

proposals.title: Use array slicing as interim workaround
proposals.description: Callers can use Array.Copy or LINQ to create sub-arrays before passing to CreateCopy. This works but incurs extra allocations the enhancement aims to eliminate.

resolution.recommendedReason: Aligns with maintainer preference expressed in #2432, follows modern .NET idioms, solves the slicing problem naturally, and requires no native code changes.

actionability.reason: Well-specified enhancement with clear implementation path. Maintainer has expressed preference for Span approach. Partially overlaps with open #2431.

actions.description: Apply enhancement and core SkiaSharp labels
Cross-reference #2431 which requests the same offset/count functionality
Acknowledge the request and share implementation direction based on maintainer feedback
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed feature request. This overlaps with #2431, and a PR (#2432) was previously submitted but closed in favor of a `ReadOnlySpan<T>`-based approach.

The planned direction is:
1. **Span overloads for `CreateCopy`** — `ReadOnlySpan<SKPoint>`, `ReadOnlySpan<SKColor>`, `ReadOnlySpan<ushort>` parameters would naturally support slicing without needing explicit offset/count parameters. The underlying C API already accepts explicit counts, so no native changes are needed.
2. **`SkVertices::Builder` exposure** — This would require new C API functions (`sk_vertices_builder_*`) and a corresponding `SKVerticesBuilder` C# class. This is a larger effort but would enable the mutable construction pattern.

In the meantime, array slicing (`AsSpan(offset, count).ToArray()`) can be used as a workaround.
